generated_oml:
instance TemperatureSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Temperature sensor defined in enriched BIM model; measures indoor air temperature and reports via Hono to Ditto."
    DTDFVocab:producedData TemperatureDataTransmitted
]

instance LightLevelSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Photometric sensor capturing light level derived from Revit photometric metadata; transmits data to Ditto."
    DTDFVocab:producedData LightLevelDataTransmitted
]

instance HVACPressureSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Pressure sensor embedded in HVAC subsystem that reports airflow pressure; data streamed to Ditto."
    DTDFVocab:producedData HVACPressureDataTransmitted
]

instance TemperatureDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom TemperatureSensor
]

instance LightLevelDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom LightLevelSensor
]

instance HVACPressureDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom HVACPressureSensor
]

instance TemperatureData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Structured temperature data record used for monitoring and analytics; annotated with timestamp and room ID."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DittoControllerEnabler
    DTDFVocab:fromData TemperatureDataTransmitted
]

instance LightLevelData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Structured light level data used for control and visualization; includes luminance, temperature, and RGB values."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DittoControllerEnabler
    DTDFVocab:fromData LightLevelDataTransmitted
]

instance HVACPressureData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Structured HVAC pressure sample used for predictive analytics; includes pressure, flow rate, timestamp."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DittoControllerEnabler
    DTDFVocab:fromData HVACPressureDataTransmitted
]

instance DittoControllerEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "DittoController that converts enriched BIM artefacts into DittoDevice objects and DittoConnection links; validates and publishes device configs."
    DTDFVocab:enables MonitoringService, ControlService, VisualizationService, PredictiveAnalyticsService, OptimizationService
]

instance BIMDeviceModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "BIM-derived device model containing metadata for devices, sensors, actuators, and control logic; consumed by DittoController."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DittoControllerEnabler
]

instance MonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Real‑time monitoring service ingesting sensor telemetry and presenting live dashboards."
    DTDFVocab:provides RuntimeTelemetryInsight
]

instance ControlService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Control service providing remote actuation of building subsystems via Ditto message bus."
    DTDFVocab:provides RemoteControlAction
]

instance VisualizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Visualization service delivering immersive 3D/UI representations of building state and simulation scenarios."
    DTDFVocab:provides VisualizationInsight
]

instance PredictiveAnalyticsService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Predictive analytics service forecasting energy use, comfort, and equipment reliability based on historical data."
    DTDFVocab:provides PredictiveEnergyInsight
]

instance OptimizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Optimization service recommending operating schedules and retrofit solutions through model-driven analysis."
    DTDFVocab:provides ScheduleOptimizationAction
]

instance RuntimeTelemetryInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insight summarizing current sensor telemetry levels for dashboards."
]

instance RemoteControlAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Action enabling remote toggling of lighting state, brightness, and color temperature via Ditto commands."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance VisualizationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insight rendering live 3D building visualization enriched with telemetry overlays."
]

instance PredictiveEnergyInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insight forecasting future energy consumption of HVAC and lighting."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance ScheduleOptimizationAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Action that recommends and applies optimized operating schedules to boost energy efficiency."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance LightTwinComponent : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Acting component that receives command events via Ditto and controls physical lamp's on/off, brightness, and color temperature."
]

instance HVACTwinComponent : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Acting component that receives HVAC control commands via Ditto and actuates HVAC hardware; follows publish/subscribe pattern."
]

instance BuildingComponent : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "BIM-based building model enriched with device definitions and architectural geometry."
]

instance HVACSubsystemComponent : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "HVAC subsystem comprising actuators, sensors, and control logic."
]

instance LightingSubsystemComponent : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Lighting subsystem with LightTwinComponent bridging physical lamps to UE5 actors."
]

instance LivingLabEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains BuildingComponent, HVACSubsystemComponent, LightingSubsystemComponent, LightTwinComponent, HVACTwinComponent, TemperatureSensor, LightLevelSensor, HVACPressureSensor
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin operates on a real‑time data‑exchange loop that continuously synchronises states between the physical building and its virtual counterpart. The framework delivers real‑time monitoring and control, with the data exchange layer guaranteeing immediate propagation of sensor updates and actuator commands across the twin. No fixed periodicity is imposed; the system reacts to incoming messages as they arrive, ensuring tight temporal coupling."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The system follows a hub‑and‑spoke design in which a central DT hub orchestrates data flow, state management, and service registration. Enriched BIM models are processed through model‑driven engineering (MDE) to automatically generate deployable DT artefacts. The architecture exposes modular extension points for analytics, control logic, and third‑party services, while runtime configuration management enables adaptation without full redeployment, ensuring coherent system‑wide coordination."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "The twin lifecycle begins with the BIM‑enriched modeling phase in which Building Control Engineers embed sensor, actuator, and controller definitions directly into the BIM model. A model‑driven engineering (MDE) engine then automatically extracts the enriched BIM and transforms it into a semantically annotated, deployable artifact that is immediately pushed to the DT infrastructure. Deployment is automated via a reproducible pipeline (R2), requiring minimal manual intervention and enabling rapid provisioning of the runtime Twin. At runtime the Twin receives continuous real‑time telemetry through the Eclipse Hono/Ditto integration (R3), maintaining a live, synchronized state with the physical building. The Twin is subsequently monitored and visualized through immersive dashboards, with control logic and custom services updated through the modular, extension‑point architecture (R4). The transition between the model and runtime stages is managed by the MDE extraction pipeline, which can re‑run whenever the BIM model changes, ensuring gated governance of changes."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "The synchronization quality between the Digital Twin and the physical system is quantified using the Overall Digital Twin Entanglement (ODTE) metric. ODTE is defined as the product of three components: Timeliness, Reliability, and Availability. In the reported living‑lab experiment, Timeliness was 0.997—measured as the delay between a physical change and its Twin update—Reliability was 1.0—the ratio of successfully received state updates to expected updates—and Availability was 1.0, indicating 100% uptime of the physical counterpart. The resulting ODTE score was 0.997, computed over a 12‑minute continuous measurement window with a 1‑second heartbeat cadence. These metrics demonstrate near‑ideal entanglement and are supported by Eclipse Hono’s at‑least‑once delivery semantics and Ditto’s persistent event storage, which mitigate message loss and support fault tolerance."
]

instance horizontal_integration : DTDFVocab:HoriIntegration [
    base:desc "Integration with external enterprise systems is achieved through open, industry‑standard protocols and adapters. The framework exposes Zigbee, OPC‑UA, and MQTT connectors to capture low‑level physical data, while higher‑level events are forwarded to external BIM repositories via 3D IFC/IFC‑XML over HTTPS. Interoperability with other Digital Twins—such as those implemented with Eclipse Ditto—occurs through a shared message bus (Eclipse Hono) that routes event streams using JSON payloads conforming to the OCF/CoAP schema. Corporate enterprise resource planning (ERP) or facility management software can hook into the platform via REST/GraphQL endpoints for analytics or control commands. The integration pattern is primarily a publish/subscribe bus for real‑time data, coupled with RESTful CRUD interfaces for configuration and historical data access."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "The DT framework integrates open‑source component stacks such as Eclipse Ditto and BaSyx, both of which provide standardized data models and connectivity interfaces for device registry and telemetry. The modular architecture exposes well‑defined extension points, enabling interoperability with BIM via generic IFC‑based semantic annotations and allowing custom plugins to conform to industry‑wide data schemas. The documented gaps in standardized data schemas and integration frameworks highlighted in related work underscore the framework’s emphasis on adopting these established standards to achieve cross‑system compatibility."
]
